subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: triangulated across multilateral bodies (IMF Article IV, OECD, Eurostat, RSF, World Justice Project, V-Dem) + French official (INSEE, Banque de France, Cour des comptes) + French press (Le Monde, Les Echos) + rating agencies.
The fiscal deficit/debt and the post-2024 budget-and-government paralysis are a single, reinforcing core negative; institutions and the economy outside the budget remain strong.
Source-lean: low-to-moderate; Anglophone wires framed the political crisis with more drama than French/multilateral sources.

economic.notes: Strong banks and controlled inflation within the eurozone, against a deficit/debt problem entangled with budget paralysis.

fiscal_state.notes: The core negative: a deficit ~5% GDP and debt ~115%+ and rising, under the EU excessive-deficit procedure, with repeated 2024-2025 budget crises and rating downgrades; consolidation is blocked by a hung parliament. (IMF Art IV May-2026; Cour des comptes; S&P/Fitch)

monetary_independence.notes: Eurozone - the ECB is credible and independent; no national control, with the Banque de France a key voice. (ECB)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: Eurozone member; the euro is the #2 global reserve currency, a modest shared benefit. (IMF COFER)

inflation.notes: Well-controlled, near the ~2% target, among the lowest in the eurozone. (Banque de France, Eurostat)

banking_stability.notes: Four global systemically important banks with solid solvency and liquidity; the sovereign-debt overhang is the main watch-item. (S&P; ACPR)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: Aerospace, luxury, agri, defence, and nuclear export strength against a persistent goods deficit; stable, predictable trade policy; no resource curse. (Banque de France)

institutional.notes: Strong courts and independent statistics, with press-ownership concentration and reform-delivery gridlock the drags.

rule_of_law.notes: Strong independent courts and a Constitutional Council; secure property and contract. (WJP; EU Rule of Law Report)

statistical_integrity.notes: INSEE is independent, Eurostat-compliant, and highly credible. (Eurostat, INSEE)

civil_service_capacity.notes: A capable, technocratic administration; reform delivery is hampered by political gridlock, not competence. (OECD)

press_freedom.notes: RSF 25th (2025, 'satisfactory'); the real drag is heavy ownership concentration (the Bollore/Vivendi galaxy) and SLAPPs. (RSF)

political_social.notes: A hung parliament producing serial government collapses, with recurring social unrest, against strong civil liberties.

political_stability.notes: The 2024 snap election produced a hung National Assembly; serial prime-ministerial collapses and budget paralysis through 2024-2025 left the president weakened, with the far right and hard left strong - but institutions hold and transfers are peaceful. (Le Monde; France 24)

civil_liberties.notes: Strong speech/assembly/due process and a robust (if confrontational) protest space; surveillance and protest-policing critiques. (V-Dem; Freedom House)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: Lawful, durable skilled pathways (the long-stay visa, the multi-year Talent permit, the EU Blue Card), but the restrictive 2024 immigration law lengthened the residence requirement and added a language test from 2026 - the skilled route stays open while the rest tightens. (France-Visas; Fragomen)

social_cohesion.notes: Recurring high-intensity strikes and protests, 2023 banlieue riots, and secularism flashpoints; a 2025 pension-reform suspension eased one fault line. (Le Monde)

demographics.notes: Fertility ~1.56 (healthier than EU peers but the lowest since WWI) and a first post-war negative natural balance in 2025; population still grows via migration. (INSEE 2025)

geopolitical.notes: A leading NATO/EU and nuclear power; an orderly but costly Sahel withdrawal completed in 2025.

alliance_reliability.notes: A NATO/EU core member, the sole EU nuclear power, and a UN Security Council permanent member - a pillar of European security. (France Diplomatie)

conflict_involvement.notes: No direct conflict and sustained Ukraine support; an orderly but strategically costly withdrawal from the Sahel completed in 2025. (UCDP)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: EU sanctions regime; no capital controls; full financial integration. (EU)

physical_practical.notes: World-class infrastructure and a strong universal health system, against intensifying climate stress.

climate.notes: Two-faced - temperate/high-readiness north vs Mediterranean-south heat/drought/wildfire and nuclear river-cooling stress; net long down. (ND-GAIN; EEA)

healthcare.notes: Among the strongest universal systems with low avoidable mortality, against access strain (medical deserts, ER pressure). (OECD/EU Health Profile)

infrastructure.notes: Excellent high-speed rail, a reliable nuclear-backed grid (a net power exporter), and strong ports/broadband/water. (RTE)

crime_safety.notes: Moderate; homicide ~1.5/100k (a rising trend, partly drug-trafficking violence), persistent urban/petty crime, and a real terrorism history. (UNODC)

disaster_insurance.notes: The CatNat regime (a state-backed mandatory natural-catastrophe scheme) provides universal access; a 2025 surcharge hike reflects rising climate losses but the scheme functions. (CCR)

summary: Strong institutions, world-class infrastructure (high-speed rail, a low-carbon nuclear grid), one of the strongest universal health systems, a leading NATO/EU and nuclear power, and controlled inflation. The dominant drag is a reinforcing fiscal-political crisis: a ~5%-of-GDP deficit and rising debt under the EU excessive-deficit procedure, with a hung parliament producing serial government collapses and budget paralysis. Recurring social unrest and press-ownership concentration are secondary negatives.
